name: Solomon Islands
avoid: November–March is wetter and more cyclone-prone, so Solomon Airlines schedules, boat crossings, and lagoon visibility are less reliable than in the April–October dry season.
Cash matters outside Honiara because kastom fees, village boat transfers, roadside food, and many island guesthouses do not reliably take cards.
Inter-island travel is the bottleneck: keep a same-day buffer before your international flight because domestic aviation and open-boat transfers shift with weather and loading.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, base in Honiara and add only one Western Province or Marovo extension; trying to squeeze Guadalcanal history, Munda/Gizo, and Seghe transfers into one short trip makes the routing too boat-and-flight heavy.

cities.name: Honiara
cities.notes: The capital is the practical entry point for Our Telekom SIMs, cash, taxis, war-memorial viewpoints, and the shortest transfer out to Guadalcanal's Bonegi wrecks and west-coast battlefields.

cities.name: Guadalcanal
cities.notes: Guadalcanal is the history-and-diving block for Bonegi I and II, Vilu War Museum, Bloody Ridge, Red Beach, and memorial stops linked to the island's WWII campaign.

cities.name: Western Province (Munda/Gizo)
cities.notes: Munda and Gizo are the easy Western Province pair for Solomon Airlines hops, Skull Island visits, Kennedy Island boat trips, Peter Joseph's WWII collection, and resort-based snorkeling or diving.

cities.name: Marovo Lagoon
cities.notes: Marovo Lagoon is the remote finale for Seghe boat transfers, village canoe outings, Uepi reefs, and low-key eco-lodges around one of the world's largest saltwater lagoons.

itinerary.sim: Our Telekom prepaid is the safest tourist SIM for Honiara, Munda, Gizo, and Seghe-area travel, while bmobile is the backup in the capital; buy and top up in Honiara before you head out to Western Province or Marovo Lagoon.

Honiara.name: Honiara
Honiara.note: Keep Honiara as the logistics-and-orientation block: one city day for memorials and market rhythm, then one buffer day before you commit to Guadalcanal drives or outer-island flights.

days.theme: Memorial Hilltops, Market Time and a Simple Capital Arrival

activities.name: Taxi rides between Henderson Airport, central Honiara, and memorial stops
activities.tip: Short Honiara taxi hops commonly land around 30–70 SBD, so carry small notes rather than relying on card payments.

activities.name: Guadalcanal American Memorial and Skyline Ridge viewpoints
activities.tip: Go near sunset for the best city-and-harbour views, then continue down into town for dinner.

activities.name: Solomon Islands National Museum and Cultural Centre
activities.tip: Entry is typically modest and is the easiest first-stop primer on local history, carvings, and shell money before you head into the field.

activities.name: Honiara Central Market walk

days.theme: Peace Park, Seafront Dining and a Flexible Pre-Island Buffer

activities.name: Solomon Peace Memorial Park
Waterfront taxi loop via Point Cruz and Mendana Avenue
activities.tip: This is the easiest low-effort way to orient yourself before an early Solomon Airlines departure the next morning.

activities.name: Our Telekom SIM and top-up in town
activities.tip: SIMs are cheap, but add a decent data pack in Honiara because coverage and recharge options get thinner once you leave the capital.

Guadalcanal.name: Guadalcanal
Guadalcanal.note: Treat Guadalcanal as a guided day-trip or overnight extension from Honiara: the best version pairs one WWII circuit with one Bonegi wreck day rather than trying to cover every battlefield marker.

days.theme: Bonegi Wrecks, West-Coast History and Beach Access

activities.name: Taxi or private driver from Honiara to Bonegi Beach
activities.tip: Bonegi sits roughly 30 minutes west of Honiara, so this works best with a half-day driver who can also continue to Vilu.

activities.name: Bonegi I & II beach access fee
activities.tip: The local access fee is commonly 50 SBD per person and is paid directly to caretakers or landowners at the site.

activities.name: Guided Bonegi shore dive with tanks and weights
activities.tip: Operator-led shore dives to the Mbonege wrecks are far pricier than simple beach access but include guide support and gear logistics.

activities.name: Vilu War Museum entry
activities.tip: The open-air relic museum usually charges about 100 SBD, and it pairs naturally with Bonegi on the same west-coast run.

days.theme: Bloody Ridge, Red Beach and a Full Guadalcanal WWII Circuit

activities.name: Guided Guadalcanal WWII full-day tour including Bloody Ridge and Red Beach
activities.tip: Organized battlefield circuits commonly land near 950 SBD per person and normally need at least two travellers to run smoothly.

activities.name: Bloody Ridge battlefield stop on a driver-led circuit
activities.tip: If you skip the full package, many drivers price Bloody Ridge as part of a custom west- or east-side history loop instead of a standalone ticket.

activities.name: Guadalcanal American Memorial revisit or sunset stop

Western Province (Munda/Gizo).name: Western Province (Munda/Gizo)
Western Province (Munda/Gizo).note: Use either Munda or Gizo as the main sleep base, then day-trip the other side by boat; the sweet spot is one history-or-snorkel day and one Skull Island or Kennedy Island outing.

days.theme: Solomon Airlines Hop, Roviana Lagoon Views and WWII Collections

activities.name: Solomon Airlines flight from Honiara to Munda or Gizo
activities.tip: One-way domestic fares on the western routes usually sit around 900–1,300 SBD, with the best availability when booked well ahead in dry season.

activities.name: Boat or taxi transfer between airstrip, lodge, and nearby reef stops
activities.tip: Short harbour transfers are often cash-only and may be folded into your lodge bill if you prearrange them.

activities.name: Peter Joseph WWII Museum
activities.tip: The privately built Munda museum is usually donation-based or modestly ticketed, and it makes the best first-stop primer before you head to larger battle sites.

days.theme: Skull Island Kastom Visit and a Kennedy Island Boat Day

activities.name: Skull Island day tour from Munda or Gizo
activities.tip: Most lodge-arranged boat days bundle fuel, guide time, and at least one snorkel stop, so always ask whether lunch is included.

activities.name: Skull Island kastom fee
activities.tip: A separate community fee of roughly 50–100 SBD is commonly paid to the island custodians even when the boat itself is prepaid.

activities.name: Kennedy Island boat excursion
activities.tip: This is an easy add-on from Gizo and is usually combined with reef swims or a longer island-hopping loop.

Marovo Lagoon.name: Marovo Lagoon
Marovo Lagoon.note: Marovo works best as the slowest block of the trip: one transfer day with a short paddle or snorkel, then one full canoe-and-village day before you route back toward Seghe.

days.theme: Seghe Transfer, Uepi Reefs and a Lagoon Arrival

activities.name: Domestic flight to Seghe or routed boat transfer into Marovo Lagoon
activities.tip: If you route via Honiara, Seghe fares are usually comparable to the main western domestic sectors; lodge-arranged boats from Seghe are priced separately.

activities.name: Boat transfer from Seghe to your lodge
activities.tip: Transfer pricing depends on fuel and distance, so confirm whether your lodge quotes per person or per boat.

activities.name: House-reef snorkel at Uepi or nearby lagoon edge

days.theme: Canoes, Village Visits and a Full Marovo Lagoon Day

activities.name: Marovo Lagoon guided canoe and snorkel day trip
activities.tip: Local canoe-and-snorkel outings commonly land around 50–100 USD per person once fuel, guide time, and lunch are included.

activities.name: Village visit or shell-money craft stop
activities.tip: Many lodges treat this as a small kastom contribution rather than a formal attraction ticket.

activities.name: Kayak or outrigger canoe hire
